Original_FR: "if the item the user wants is available for exchange, the user can request the exchange of items that he/she has with the item he/she wants."
Conflicting_FR: Users can request exchanges only if the items are unavailable.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the term "unavailable" in Requirement B to specify whether it refers to items being out of stock, not listed for exchange, or otherwise inaccessible, and align it with Requirement A by allowing exchanges only when the desired item is available but the user's offered item is unavailable for direct trade.  
2. Merge the requirements by establishing a two-step process: users first check availability of the desired item (per Requirement A), and if unavailable, they can still request an exchange but are notified of potential delays or conditions (per Requirement B).  
3. Prioritize the requirements based on system goals—e.g., if facilitating exchanges is primary, adopt Requirement A and revise Requirement B to apply only to specific item categories or user tiers where availability restrictions are necessary.

Original_FR: "if the user no longer wishes to sell/exchange the item, he/she can remove the item from the system by touching a??Remove the itema?? button."
Conflicting_FR: Items shall remain in the system even if the user clicks "Remove the item".
Resolution:
1. Introduce a soft delete mechanism where items are marked as "removed" or "archived" but remain in the system for record-keeping, while being hidden from the user's active listings.
2. Clarify the scope of "remove" by distinguishing between removing an item from public view or exchange listings versus deleting it entirely from the database, and update Requirement A to specify the former.
3. Add a user confirmation step with an explanation that the item will be retained in the system for historical or administrative purposes, ensuring transparency and aligning both requirements.

Original_FR: "if the user no longer wishes to use the system, he/she can click a??Delete Profilea?? button. After deleting the profile, the user will no longer be able to use the system with the account details that he entered before."
Conflicting_FR: The system shall retain user account and allow login even after clicking "Delete Profile".
Resolution:
1. Introduce a "soft delete" feature where clicking "Delete Profile" deactivates the account for user access but retains data for a specified period (e.g., 30 days) before permanent deletion, allowing reversible action.  
2. Clarify terminology by renaming the button to "Deactivate Profile" for temporary suspension and adding a separate "Permanently Delete Account" option in settings for irreversible removal.  
3. Implement a confirmation step after clicking "Delete Profile" that explains data retention policies and offers choices: delete immediately or retain account with login disabled for future recovery.

Original_FR: 10000 users should be able to use the system at the same time.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall restrict the number of concurrent users to a maximum of 5000 to maintain response time and data consistency.
Resolution:
1. Implement a tiered access system that allows up to 5000 concurrent users with full functionality while enabling an additional 5000 users in a read-only or limited mode to meet the 10000 total concurrent target without compromising performance.

2. Scale system capacity through load balancing and performance optimization (e.g., caching, database tuning) to support 10000 concurrent users while maintaining acceptable response times and data consistency, then adjust Requirement B accordingly.

3. Introduce a dynamic queuing or scheduling mechanism that prioritizes user access, ensuring no more than 5000 users are active simultaneously but allowing up to 10000 to be "logged in" or waiting with minimal resource use.

Original_FR: 10000 users should be able to use the system at the same time.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall allow administrative concurrent access only once per system startup and revoke all administrative sessions automatically within 5 seconds after they begin.
Resolution:
1. Clarify that Requirement A refers to general user concurrency, while Requirement B applies only to administrative sessions, and explicitly separate the user and administrative access limits in the system design.  
2. Modify Requirement B to allow administrative sessions to be limited in number (e.g., only one at a time) but not tied strictly to system startup, ensuring general user concurrency (Requirement A) is unaffected.  
3. Introduce a session management mechanism that distinguishes between regular users and administrators, ensuring administrative sessions are automatically terminated within 5 seconds without impacting the 10,000 concurrent user capacity.

Original_FR: A CEP card may contain currencies that the load acquirer does not support. As a result, load devices must use the alphabetic issuer-supplied currency code and currency exponent from the CEP slot(s) to display the source amounts for the currency conversion transaction.
Conflicting_FR: Load devices shall reject any CEP card containing currencies not supported by the load acquirer, and no source amount shall be displayed.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement B to allow the load device to display the source amount using the issuer-supplied currency code and exponent, but then reject the transaction before processing, ensuring the user is informed but no unsupported currency is accepted.
2. Update Requirement A to specify that the display of source amounts for unsupported currencies is only for informational or error messaging purposes, and the transaction must ultimately be rejected as per Requirement B.
3. Introduce a conditional rule: if a CEP card contains an unsupported currency, the load device first displays the source amount (as per Requirement A) with a clear warning, then automatically rejects the transaction (as per Requirement B), ensuring both informational and security needs are met.

Original_FR: A class shall be either a non-clinical class or a clinical class.
Conflicting_FR: A class may contain both clinical and non-clinical components within the same structure.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the definition of "class" by distinguishing between a class as a whole entity and its internal components, allowing Requirement A to refer to the overall classification while Requirement B describes permissible internal composition.  
2. Introduce a third category, such as "hybrid class," in Requirement A to accommodate classes that contain both clinical and non-clinical components, aligning it with Requirement B.  
3. Revise Requirement B to specify that a class may contain mixed components only if it is explicitly designated as an exception, thereby preserving Requirement A’s primary classification while allowing flexibility under defined conditions.

Original_FR: A CND as defined in clause 3 may use the CNG to access NGN services.
Conflicting_FR: A CND shall not be allowed to access NGN services through the CNG interface for security reasons.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the definition of "CND" in clause 3 to specify whether it includes or excludes scenarios where security restrictions apply, and update Requirement A to reflect conditional access.  
2. Introduce a security exception clause in Requirement B, allowing CND access through CNG only after meeting specific authentication or authorization criteria.  
3. Merge both requirements into a single, precise statement: "A CND as defined in clause 3 may use the CNG to access NGN services, provided that security controls defined in [reference] are satisfied."

Original_FR: A cohort shall be associated to one Program of Study offered by the department.
Conflicting_FR: A cohort may be associated with multiple Programs of Study across departments.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the definition of "cohort" to determine if it refers to a group of students within a single academic track or a broader administrative grouping that can span multiple programs, then adjust one requirement to align with the clarified scope.

2. Introduce a hierarchical or multi-level association model where a cohort is primarily linked to one main Program of Study but can have secondary or optional associations with other Programs of Study for cross-departmental collaboration.

3. Specify contextual conditions: require that a cohort is associated with one Program of Study within its home department by default, but allow exceptions for interdisciplinary cohorts that can be associated with multiple Programs of Study upon approval.

Original_FR: A cohort shall be associated to one Program of Study offered by the department.
Conflicting_FR: A cohort's Program of Study may be changed only once per calendar year and any change must be applied within a 10-second transaction window; changes attempted outside this window shall be rejected even if performed by DEPARTMENT_ADMIN.
Resolution:
1. Clarify that Requirement B’s “Program of Study may be changed only once per calendar year” refers to the cohort’s *current* association, not the initial assignment, and ensure the 10-second transaction window applies only to that annual change, not to the initial cohort-to-program linkage.

2. Introduce a system state or flag to distinguish between the initial cohort-program association (which is mandatory per Requirement A) and subsequent changes, applying the once-per-year and 10-second constraints only to the latter.

3. Specify that Requirement B’s restrictions apply only after the cohort has been initially associated with a Program of Study, and that the initial association is exempt from the once-per-year limit and transaction window, provided it occurs during cohort creation.

Original_FR: A CPN user shall be able to configure any unmanaged service provided to the user.
Conflicting_FR: A CPN user shall not be allowed to configure unmanaged services without administrator approval.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a tiered configuration model where basic, low-risk settings for unmanaged services can be configured by the user without approval, while advanced or high-impact changes require administrator review and authorization.

2. Implement a workflow where a user can request configuration changes for unmanaged services, which are then automatically logged and optionally sent for administrator approval based on predefined risk criteria (e.g., security, performance impact).

3. Clarify the scope by distinguishing between "configuration" types: allow users to configure non-critical, user-specific parameters (e.g., display settings) freely, while restricting system-level or security-related configurations to administrator-approved actions only.

Original_FR: A current temperature value that is received from an individual thermostat shall be compared to the valid temperature range values.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall not perform validation against predefined range values for temperature inputs.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the scope of validation by specifying that Requirement A applies only to thermostat data for internal monitoring, while Requirement B refers to external user inputs that should not be restricted by predefined ranges.
2. Modify Requirement A to focus on logging or alerting when temperatures fall outside a range, rather than performing active validation that restricts system behavior.
3. Introduce a conditional clause in Requirement B to allow range-based checks for safety-critical thermostat readings while excluding non-critical temperature inputs from validation.

Original_FR: A description and time/date of each prescription that has been ordered or given is displayed starting with most recent.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall display prescriptions in chronological order from oldest to newest.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the primary use case and adopt a single ordering standard, such as "display prescriptions in chronological order from oldest to newest," with an optional toggle or filter to view the most recent first when needed.  
2. Separate the display into two distinct views or sections: one showing a chronological list (oldest to newest) and another showing a summary or highlighted section with only the most recent prescriptions.  
3. Define "starting with most recent" as the default sort order for a specific user role or context (e.g., quick review), while maintaining an option to switch to a full chronological list for detailed historical tracking.

Original_FR: A failed login will alert the physician/pharmacist.
Conflicting_FR: A failed login attempt shall not generate alerts to users for security reasons.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a tiered alert system where failed logins trigger internal security alerts for administrators or IT staff, but not for general users like physicians or pharmacists, thereby separating operational monitoring from user notifications.
2. Modify Requirement A to specify that only repeated or suspicious patterns of failed logins (e.g., multiple attempts from an unfamiliar location) generate alerts to authorized personnel, while single common failures remain silent to users.
3. Clarify the stakeholder intent by distinguishing between "security alerts" (which may log events for audit purposes without user disruption) and "user notifications," ensuring Requirement B prevents pop-ups or emails to users while Requirement A can be satisfied through secure administrative dashboards.

Original_FR: A failed login will alert the physician/pharmacist.
Conflicting_FR: Login failure alerts shall be delivered only once to Security Administrators and thereafter be suppressed for 24 hours, regardless of subsequent failures.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the alert recipient: Specify that Requirement A alerts the physician/pharmacist for their own login failures only, while Requirement B applies to Security Administrators for system-wide login failure monitoring, ensuring no overlap in alert targets.

2. Distinguish alert types: Define Requirement A as an immediate, user-facing notification (e.g., on-screen message) for the failed login attempt, and Requirement B as a separate, log-based administrative alert sent to Security Administrators, with suppression rules applied only to the latter.

3. Introduce role-based alert routing: Implement a rule where login failure alerts are sent to the user (physician/pharmacist) in real-time per Requirement A, but aggregated alerts to Security Administrators follow the 24-hour suppression policy in Requirement B, preventing duplicate administrative notifications.

Original_FR: A flight pattern shall document the longitude latitude and altitude of waypoints in a flight route as offsets from the starting position of the UAV.
Conflicting_FR: A flight pattern shall document the magnetic bearing and distance of waypoints in a flight route as offsets from the starting position of the UAV.
Resolution:
1. Combine both coordinate systems into a single requirement, specifying that the flight pattern must document waypoints using both longitude/latitude/altitude offsets and magnetic bearing/distance offsets for redundancy and flexibility.

2. Create a higher-level requirement that mandates the system to support multiple coordinate representations, then define separate sub-requirements for each format, allowing the implementation to choose based on operational context or user preference.

3. Clarify the operational context and stakeholder needs to determine which representation is primary; then designate one as the mandatory format and the other as an optional or derived data field that can be calculated from the primary.

Original_FR: A flight pattern shall document the longitude latitude and altitude of waypoints in a flight route as offsets from the starting position of the UAV.
Conflicting_FR: A flight pattern shall record waypoints using absolute geographic coordinates rather than offsets.
Resolution:
1. Combine both approaches by storing waypoints as absolute coordinates but also calculating and logging the offsets for internal use, ensuring the system meets both documentation and recording needs.  
2. Clarify the stakeholder intent: determine if Requirement A is for simplified pilot/operator documentation while Requirement B is for precise navigation systems, then split the requirements accordingly.  
3. Introduce a configuration option allowing the user to choose between offset-based or absolute coordinate recording, with clear documentation on when each mode is appropriate.

Original_FR: A list of all dispute cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color-coding the line item in the list.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall provide a textual indication of case status instead of using color-coding.
Resolution:
1. Combine both approaches by using color-coding for case type and textual labels for case status, satisfying both requirements for distinct attributes.
2. Implement a toggle feature allowing users to switch between a color-coded view and a text-only view based on their preference or accessibility needs.
3. Use color-coding as a secondary visual enhancement while ensuring primary status information is always conveyed through clear, mandatory text labels.

Original_FR: A list of all dispute cases that meet the entered search criteria must be provided to the user. The list of cases must differentiate the type and/or status of the case by color-coding the line item in the list.
Conflicting_FR: The list of dispute cases shall differentiate type/status using textual tags instead of color-coding.
Resolution:
1. Combine both methods by using color-coding for immediate visual differentiation and adding textual tags for accessibility and clarity, ensuring compliance with accessibility standards like WCAG.
2. Allow user customization in the system settings to choose between color-coding, textual tags, or both, based on individual preference or role requirements.
3. Implement a hybrid approach where textual tags are the primary indicator, with optional color highlights that can be toggled on or off by the user.

Original_FR: A list of available patients is displayed after login.
Conflicting_FR: The list of available patients shall be accessible only after explicit user request, not automatically after login.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that the patient list is displayed only after the user explicitly requests it, such as by clicking a button or menu option after login.  
2. Introduce a user preference setting that allows the system to either automatically display the list after login or wait for an explicit request, satisfying both optional behaviors.  
3. Redesign the login flow to show a dashboard after login, where the patient list is accessible via a clearly marked link or button, rather than appearing automatically.

Original_FR: A meeting scheduler system will inform the ???potential meeting participant???? of the meeting.
Conflicting_FR: The meeting scheduler shall notify only confirmed attendees, excluding tentative participants.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the term "potential meeting participant" in Requirement A to specify whether it includes only confirmed attendees or also tentative participants, aligning with Requirement B's explicit focus on confirmed attendees only.  
2. Introduce a two-step notification process: Requirement A could apply to initial invitations (informing potential participants), while Requirement B governs final confirmations (notifying only confirmed attendees).  
3. Revise Requirement A to explicitly state that notifications are sent to "confirmed attendees" rather than "potential meeting participants," eliminating the conflict by harmonizing terminology with Requirement B.

Original_FR: A meeting scheduler system will inform the potential meeting participant of the meeting.
Conflicting_FR: The meeting scheduler system shall not automatically notify participants until the meeting is confirmed.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the term "inform" in Requirement A to specify that notification occurs only after explicit confirmation, aligning with Requirement B's condition.  
2. Introduce a two-step notification process: first, an optional "tentative" alert when a meeting is proposed, followed by a formal confirmation notification once finalized.  
3. Define distinct user roles, allowing the meeting organizer to manually trigger participant notifications only after the meeting status is set to confirmed.

Original_FR: A meeting scheduler system will temporarily reserve the ???time slots???? for the proposed meeting.
Conflicting_FR: The scheduler shall not reserve time slots until the meeting is finalized.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a "soft hold" or "tentative reservation" state that temporarily blocks time slots without committing them, which can be automatically released if the meeting is not finalized within a set period.  
2. Clarify the requirements by specifying that time slots are only reserved after explicit user confirmation, and any earlier "reservation" is merely a visual placeholder in the interface.  
3. Modify Requirement A to state that the system will only *display* proposed time slots as available, but will not formally reserve them until the meeting is confirmed.

Original_FR: A MODIS Computer Software Configuration Item (CSCI) is any independently tracked and configured unit of software within the MODIS SDP S/W, including processes, scripts, and utility libraries.  Each CSCI in the MODIS SDP S/W shall run in the ECS production environment.
Conflicting_FR: Access to MODIS CSCI configuration data shall be permitted to MODIS_SYSTEM_ADMIN holders only during a predefined maintenance window of 2 minutes per week; outside that 2-minute window no access or modification is allowed.
Resolution:
1. Clarify that "run in the ECS production environment" refers to normal operational execution, not administrative access, and explicitly state that Requirement B's access restriction applies only to configuration data modifications, not to the CSCI's runtime operation.

2. Introduce a distinction between runtime processes and administrative interfaces, specifying that the CSCI itself runs continuously as required, but the configuration management subsystem is only accessible during the designated 2-minute weekly maintenance window.

3. Amend Requirement A to include an exception clause, such as: "Each CSCI shall run in the ECS production environment, except for its configuration management functions, which are subject to the access controls specified in Requirement B."

Original_FR: A MODIS Computer Software Configuration Item (CSCI) is any independently tracked and configured unit of software within the MODIS SDP S/W, including processes, scripts, and utility libraries.  Each CSCI within the MODIS SDP S/W systemshall employ the Earth Science Data and Information System (ESDIS)-supplied SDPTK mandatory functions to access the DAAC production environment and services.
Conflicting_FR: Use of ESDIS SDPTK functions shall be rate-limited to one invocation per 24 hours per DAAC administrator; any additional attempts within the period are rejected even if initiated by DAAC admins.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to allow for optional or conditional use of ESDIS SDPTK functions, specifying that certain CSCI operations may use alternative access methods when rate limits are reached, ensuring system functionality is not blocked by the 24-hour restriction.

2. Update Requirement B to include an exception mechanism for critical or time-sensitive CSCI operations, allowing DAAC administrators to request temporary overrides or elevated quotas with proper approval and logging, balancing security with operational needs.

3. Clarify the scope of Requirement B by specifying that the rate limit applies only to non-essential or administrative invocations, while essential CSCI functions required for core MODIS data processing are exempt or subject to a separate, higher limit.

Original_FR: A MODIS CSCI ... shall be coded according to the standards specified in SDST-022.
Conflicting_FR: Each CSCI may be coded according to team-specific internal guidelines if equivalent to SDST-022.
Resolution:
1. Revise Requirement B to state that team-specific guidelines must be formally reviewed and certified as equivalent to SDST-022 by a designated authority before use.
2. Amend Requirement A to allow for deviations if an approved waiver is obtained, and update Requirement B to reference this waiver process explicitly.
3. Merge the requirements into a single, clear requirement: "Each CSCI shall be coded according to SDST-022 or an approved equivalent set of team-specific guidelines, with equivalence determined through a formal review process."

Original_FR: A MODIS CSCI ... shall employ ESDIS-supplied SDPTK mandatory functions to access the DAAC environment.
Conflicting_FR: A CSCI may use custom-developed functions to access DAAC services instead of ESDIS-supplied SDPTK.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the scope by specifying that Requirement A applies to mandatory baseline functions, while Requirement B allows custom functions for optional or extended services not covered by the SDPTK.
2. Introduce a conditional clause in Requirement B, stating that custom functions may be used only if they meet or exceed the performance, security, and interoperability standards of the ESDIS-supplied SDPTK functions.
3. Establish a governance process where any deviation from the SDPTK (as allowed by Requirement B) requires formal review and approval by the ESDIS authority to ensure compliance with system-wide integration and support requirements.

Original_FR: A MODIS CSCI ... shall run in the ECS production environment.
Conflicting_FR: Each CSCI shall run in a controlled test environment before deployment to ECS production.
Resolution:
1. Clarify that Requirement A specifies the final operational state, while Requirement B mandates a prerequisite testing phase; update Requirement B to explicitly state that successful completion of the controlled test environment is required before deployment to the ECS production environment as per Requirement A.

2. Introduce a phased deployment process in which the CSCI first runs in the controlled test environment (Requirement B) and, after verification and approval, is transitioned to the ECS production environment (Requirement A), with both requirements retained but sequenced in the project timeline.

3. Revise Requirement A to include a conditional clause, such as "A MODIS CSCI ... shall run in the ECS production environment after successful testing in a controlled test environment," thereby integrating Requirement B as a precondition and eliminating the conflict.

Original_FR: A MySQL database will be used to store all information required to answer questions.
Conflicting_FR: A PostgreSQL database shall be used as the primary storage for all question-answer data.
Resolution:
1. Choose a single database system by evaluating technical needs, such as performance, scalability, and compatibility with existing infrastructure, and then update both requirements to specify the selected system (e.g., MySQL or PostgreSQL) consistently.

2. Implement a hybrid approach where one database (e.g., PostgreSQL) serves as the primary storage for structured question-answer data, while the other (e.g., MySQL) handles auxiliary information, clearly defining the scope for each in the requirements.

3. Introduce an abstraction layer, such as a database-agnostic ORM or API, to decouple the application from a specific database, allowing either MySQL or PostgreSQL to be used interchangeably based on deployment preferences.

Original_FR: A PGE ... Each PGE shall be provided with one or more sets of production rules specifying inputs, outputs, and activation conditions.
Conflicting_FR: A PGE shall execute dynamically without predefined production rules.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the term "production rules" to distinguish between static configuration and dynamic generation, allowing Requirement B to refer to predefined static rules while Requirement A accommodates rules generated at runtime.  
2. Introduce a phased execution model where a PGE initially uses predefined rules for setup (satisfying Requirement A) but can later operate with dynamically generated or modified rules (aligning with Requirement B).  
3. Reframe the requirements hierarchically: define a base PGE that supports production rules (Requirement A) and a specialized subtype that enables dynamic execution without predefined rules (Requirement B), ensuring both can coexist in the system.

Original_FR: A PGE ... shall not set or redefine environmental variables.
Conflicting_FR: A PGE shall be permitted to modify environmental variables during execution for configuration purposes.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the scope by distinguishing between "setting/redefining" and "modifying" environmental variables, specifying that a PGE may adjust predefined configuration variables but not introduce new ones or alter core system variables.
2. Introduce a controlled exception list, allowing modifications only for specific, pre-approved configuration variables while maintaining a general prohibition on setting or redefining environmental variables.
3. Reframe Requirement A to prohibit persistent changes to the environment, while permitting temporary, execution-scoped modifications as allowed in Requirement B.

Original_FR: A PGE ... The SDST shall integrate all software processes into PGEs executing all processes in the MODIS TLCF.
Conflicting_FR: The SDST shall integrate only selected software processes into PGEs, not all processes.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the scope of "all processes" by defining which software processes are mandatory for integration and which are optional, aligning Requirement A with a core subset and Requirement B with additional selective processes.  
2. Introduce a phased integration approach where all processes are integrated initially (per Requirement A) but with a configuration option to disable or exclude non-essential processes as needed (addressing Requirement B).  
3. Revise the requirements to distinguish between "critical" processes (must be integrated) and "ancillary" processes (selectively integrated), merging both into a single, prioritized requirement.

Original_FR: A PGE ... The SDST shall integrate subsets of these processes into sets of PGEs that execute in DAAC centers.
Conflicting_FR: All PGEs shall execute within a unified ECS environment instead of separate DAAC centers.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the scope of "unified ECS environment" to allow for distributed execution across DAAC centers while maintaining a consistent operational framework, ensuring Requirement B's intent of a unified environment is met without excluding DAAC-specific deployments as indicated in Requirement A.

2. Revise Requirement A to specify that PGEs are integrated and managed centrally within the unified ECS environment, but their execution can be logically distributed or assigned to DAAC centers as needed, aligning both requirements under a single architectural model.

3. Introduce a tiered execution model where core PGEs run in the unified ECS environment for consistency, while specialized or data-local subsets may execute at DAAC centers with strict integration and interoperability standards to satisfy both requirements.

Original_FR: A PGE is a script which executes one or more MODIS software processes within the ECS environment.  A PGE shall not set or redefine environmental variables.
Conflicting_FR: A PGE executed by a PROCESS_ADMIN may set or redefine environment variables only during its first execution; subsequent executions by any PROCESS_ADMIN shall be prohibited from changing environment variables.
Resolution:
1. Clarify that Requirement A applies to PGEs in general, while Requirement B is a specific exception for PROCESS_ADMIN executions, and update Requirement A to explicitly state: "A PGE shall not set or redefine environmental variables, except as permitted under specific administrative conditions defined elsewhere."

2. Merge the two requirements into a single, unambiguous statement: "A PGE is a script which executes one or more MODIS software processes within the ECS environment. Under normal operation, a PGE shall not set or redefine environmental variables; however, when executed by a PROCESS_ADMIN, it may set or redefine environment variables only during its first execution, with subsequent executions prohibited from making changes."

3. Introduce a conditional clause in Requirement B to align with Requirement A by specifying that the ability to set variables is limited to a designated initialization phase, after which all PGEs, including those run by PROCESS_ADMIN, must adhere to the no-set rule, thereby preserving system consistency.

Original_FR: A Program Administrator/Nursing Staff Member shall be able to add a new nursing student to the system.
Conflicting_FR: Only the System Administrator shall be permitted to add new nursing students.
Resolution:
1. Define distinct user roles and permissions, allowing a Program Administrator or Nursing Staff Member to initiate a student addition request, which must then be approved by the System Administrator before finalizing.
2. Implement a tiered access system where the System Administrator retains the primary ability to add students, but can delegate temporary or limited permissions to specific Program Administrators for defined periods or batches.
3. Clarify the requirements by specifying that "add a new nursing student" in Requirement A refers to inputting preliminary data or creating a draft record, while Requirement B refers to the final, authoritative activation of the student account in the system.

Original_FR: A Program of Study shall consist of a program name and listing of required classes.
Conflicting_FR: A Program of Study may omit required classes and include elective components only.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the definition of "required classes" to allow for programs that are entirely elective-based, possibly by redefining Requirement A to state that a Program of Study consists of a program name and a listing of classes, which may be designated as required or elective.

2. Introduce a program type or category system where some programs (e.g., "Structured Programs") must include required classes as per Requirement A, while others (e.g., "Flexible Programs") may consist solely of elective components as per Requirement B.

3. Modify Requirement B to specify that elective-only programs are a special case, requiring an explicit exception or override in the system, ensuring that Requirement A remains the default rule unless explicitly waived.

Original_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR602 results in a displayed set of topics.
Conflicting_FR: The search described in UCAR602 shall output a summary report instead of displaying topics.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the intended output by merging both requirements: specify that the search results in a displayed set of topics with an option to generate a summary report from those topics.
2. Define distinct user scenarios: use the displayed topics for interactive browsing and the summary report for a printable or exportable overview, triggered by separate user actions.
3. Prioritize based on stakeholder feedback: determine whether the primary need is real-time topic exploration (Requirement A) or a consolidated output (Requirement B), and adjust the requirement accordingly.

Original_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR602 results in a displayed set of topics.
Conflicting_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR602 shall output a summary report instead of displaying topics.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a user-selectable output mode that allows the search to either display a set of topics or generate a summary report, based on user preference or context.
2. Define a two-step process where the search first displays topics, then offers an option to generate a summary report from the selected topics.
3. Clarify the requirement scope by specifying that UCAR602 refers to two distinct search types: one for topic discovery and another for report generation, each with separate triggers or use cases.

Original_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR702 results in a displayed set of topics.
Conflicting_FR: The search described in UCAR702 shall return topic identifiers only, not full topic details.
Resolution:
1. Clarify that the displayed set of topics in Requirement A shows only topic identifiers, aligning with Requirement B, and update the wording to specify that no full details are shown.  
2. Introduce a two-step process where the search returns identifiers (per Requirement B) and then allows the user to select an identifier to view full details separately, satisfying both requirements.  
3. Revise Requirement A to state that the search results display a list of topic identifiers, explicitly removing any implication of showing full topic details.

Original_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR702 results in a displayed set of topics.
Conflicting_FR: A search described in requirement UCAR702 shall return only topic identifiers, not full topic details.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the term "displayed set of topics" in Requirement A to specify that only topic identifiers are shown initially, with an option to retrieve full details upon user selection.  
2. Update Requirement B to allow returning minimal topic details (e.g., identifier and title) for display purposes, while deferring full details until explicitly requested.  
3. Introduce a two-step search process where the first step returns only identifiers (per Requirement B) and the second step fetches and displays full details only for user-selected topics (aligning with Requirement A).

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR201 and UCAR202 results in a displayed set of buildings that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: The search described in UCAR201 and UCAR202 shall return only building IDs, not the full building list.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the output format by updating Requirement A to specify that the displayed set includes only building IDs, not full building details, ensuring both requirements align on returning IDs only.  
2. Introduce a two-step process where the search returns building IDs (per Requirement B), and then a separate user action (like clicking an ID) triggers the display of the full building list, satisfying Requirement A’s display intent.  
3. Refine the terminology in both requirements to distinguish between “returned data” (IDs) and “displayed information,” allowing the system to return IDs internally while showing a user-friendly list (e.g., IDs with names) on the interface.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR201 and UCAR202 results in a displayed set of buildings that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR201 and UCAR202 shall return only building IDs, not the full building list.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the scope of "displayed set" in Requirement A to specify that it shows only building IDs, aligning with Requirement B's output constraint.  
2. Update Requirement A to state that the search results are first returned as IDs, then optionally retrieved and displayed as full building details in a separate step.  
3. Merge both requirements by defining that the search returns building IDs, and the system can display either IDs or a summarized list based on user or system context.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR203 results in a displayed set of POI that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: If the quick search option is selected, the system shall use only the POI name field from the user input, ignore all other search parameters, and return the top 10 most frequently accessed POIs.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the relationship between UCAR203 and the quick search option, specifying that the quick search overrides standard search parameters when selected, ensuring Requirement B takes precedence over Requirement A in that scenario.  
2. Modify Requirement A to include an exception clause, such as "unless the quick search option is selected," to explicitly exclude quick searches from its scope and prevent overlap.  
3. Define "top 10 most frequently accessed POIs" in Requirement B as a subset of the POIs matching the search input in Requirement A, ensuring both requirements can be satisfied simultaneously by prioritizing quick search results within the broader matching set.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR203 results in a displayed set of POIs that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: The search described in UCAR203 shall exclude POIs from restricted areas.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the definition of "restricted areas" in Requirement B to ensure it is mutually exclusive with the "displayed set of POIs" in Requirement A, and update Requirement A to explicitly state that only non-restricted POIs are shown.  
2. Introduce a filtering step in the search process where POIs from restricted areas are excluded before the result set is displayed, ensuring both requirements are satisfied without conflict.  
3. Add a user permission or role-based access control to Requirement A, allowing authorized users to see restricted POIs while excluding them for general users, aligning with Requirement B’s intent.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR406 and UCAR407 results in a displayed set of events that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: The search described in UCAR406 and UCAR407 shall only return future events, not past events.
Resolution:
1. Clarify Requirement A to explicitly state that the displayed set of events must be limited to future events, aligning with Requirement B.
2. Update Requirement A to include a filter or toggle option allowing users to choose between viewing all matching events or only future events, thereby satisfying both requirements through user control.
3. Define "events that match the search input" in Requirement A as inherently restricted by temporal criteria, ensuring both requirements are consistent by incorporating the future-only constraint directly into the search logic.

Original_FR: A search described in requirements UCAR501 and UCAR502 results in a displayed set of employees that match the search input.
Conflicting_FR: Only HR administrators shall be able to perform searches on employees; regular users shall not access this function.
Resolution:
1. Restrict the search functionality described in UCAR501 and UCAR502 to HR administrators only, ensuring that regular users are blocked from accessing or viewing the search interface entirely.  
2. Implement role-based access control where the search results are displayed only if the user is an HR administrator; otherwise, show an error message or hide the function.  
3. Clarify the requirements by specifying that UCAR501 and UCAR502 apply only to HR administrators, and update any related documentation to reflect this access restriction.

Original_FR: A single adult shall be able to lift and carry the UAV.
Conflicting_FR: The UAV shall require at least two adults to lift and carry it safely.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the operational context: Specify whether the requirement for a single adult to lift the UAV applies to emergency or short-distance handling, while requiring two adults applies to routine transport or safety protocols, thereby distinguishing use cases.

2. Define weight thresholds: Establish that the UAV must be light enough for one adult to lift unassisted (e.g., under 25 kg) but require two adults for safe carrying over longer distances or uneven terrain, aligning both requirements through conditional clauses.

3. Merge requirements with a safety condition: Rewrite to state that the UAV shall be liftable by one adult in controlled conditions, but operational procedures must mandate two adults for carrying to ensure safety and stability during movement.

Original_FR: A single currency cannot occupy more than one slot.
Conflicting_FR: The CEP card may allow a currency to be assigned to multiple slots for redundancy.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the term "slot" to distinguish between logical assignment slots and physical redundancy slots, specifying that Requirement A applies to logical currency assignment while Requirement B addresses physical redundancy configurations.  
2. Introduce a conditional exception in Requirement A, such as "unless explicitly configured for redundancy as per the CEP card's specifications," to allow controlled multi-slot assignment under defined redundancy scenarios.  
3. Define a hierarchy or priority between the requirements, stating that Requirement B overrides Requirement A only when redundancy is enabled, and document this decision in the system's configuration guidelines.

Original_FR: A staff member shall have the ability to manage the progress of a part-time student.
Conflicting_FR: Only academic advisors shall be permitted to modify a part-time student’s class sequence.
Resolution:
1. Clarify that "manage the progress" in Requirement A excludes modifying the class sequence, limiting staff members to view-only or advisory actions such as tracking attendance or performance without altering enrollment.  
2. Define "staff member" in Requirement A to explicitly exclude academic advisors, ensuring that only designated academic advisors have modification rights as per Requirement B.  
3. Introduce a role-based permission system where staff members can manage general progress (e.g., monitoring milestones), but any changes to the class sequence require approval or action by an academic advisor.

Original_FR: A suitably authorized CPN user (e.g. the system administrator) shall be able to install and activate NGN managed services without the intervention of the subscribing CPN user.
Conflicting_FR: New NGN managed services shall be considered approved automatically if the subscribing CPN user does not respond within 3 seconds of the one-time code being issued.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the authorization scope in Requirement A to specify that automatic approval (as in Requirement B) is a valid form of "without intervention" for non-responsive users, while still requiring explicit approval for active users within the 3-second window.
2. Introduce a tiered approval process: allow immediate activation by authorized CPN users for critical services, while applying the 3-second auto-approval rule only for low-risk or pre-authorized managed services.
3. Add an explicit exception to Requirement B stating that if a CPN administrator initiates the installation, the 3-second user response window is bypassed entirely, ensuring administrative actions are not delayed.

Original_FR: A suitably authorized CPN user (e.g., the system administrator) shall be able to install and activate NGN managed services.
Conflicting_FR: Only the service provider’s technical support personnel shall be allowed to install and activate NGN managed services.
Resolution:
1. Clarify roles by defining "suitably authorized CPN user" to exclude the service provider’s technical support personnel, ensuring only one distinct group has the installation and activation rights.  
2. Introduce a tiered access model where the system administrator can install services but activation requires approval or action by the service provider’s technical support personnel.  
3. Revise the requirements to separate "install" and "activate" permissions, allowing the CPN user to install services while restricting activation exclusively to the service provider’s support team.

Original_FR: A suitably authorized CPN user shall be able to manage and configure provisioning capabilities.
Conflicting_FR: Only the NGN central management system shall manage provisioning; CPN users shall not have configuration rights.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the term "manage and configure provisioning capabilities" in Requirement A to specify that CPN users can only perform predefined, low-risk actions (e.g., view status or initiate pre-approved requests) while reserving full configuration rights exclusively for the NGN central management system as per Requirement B.  
2. Introduce a role-based access control system where "suitably authorized" in Requirement A is explicitly defined to exclude provisioning configuration rights, ensuring CPN users have only operational or monitoring permissions that do not conflict with Requirement B.  
3. Revise Requirement A to align with Requirement B by removing "configure" and limiting CPN user actions to "monitor" or "request provisioning," thereby eliminating the direct conflict over configuration authority.

Original_FR: A temperature setting value that is received from an individual thermostat shall be compared to the valid temperature range values.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall not perform validation against temperature range values to reduce processing overhead.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the validation scope by specifying that Requirement A applies only to initial thermostat inputs, while Requirement B pertains to subsequent internal processing, thereby eliminating redundant checks.
2. Introduce a conditional validation rule where temperature values are only compared against the valid range when they originate from an external source, and cached or previously validated values bypass further checks to reduce overhead.
3. Revise Requirement B to allow lightweight validation (e.g., boundary checks only) for critical safety or compliance scenarios, while maintaining minimal processing impact for routine operations.

Original_FR: A transaction that passes merchant acquirer validations and is forwarded to the card issuer is paid for by the card issuer.
Conflicting_FR: The acquirer shall make payment to the merchant directly without card issuer validation.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the transaction flow by specifying that Requirement A applies only to issuer-validated transactions, while Requirement B applies to a separate direct-payment mechanism for pre-approved or low-risk transactions.  
2. Introduce a conditional rule: if the transaction is validated by the card issuer, the issuer pays; otherwise, the acquirer pays the merchant directly under predefined conditions (e.g., small amounts, trusted merchants).  
3. Reframe the requirements to distinguish between payment guarantee and payment timing—Requirement A ensures issuer liability post-validation, while Requirement B ensures immediate merchant funding, with reconciliation handled later between issuer and acquirer.

Original_FR: A user can select the desired product from the results and proceed to the product details.
Conflicting_FR: Only registered users shall be allowed to access product details; guest users shall not.
Resolution:
1. Allow guest users to view basic product details but require registration for advanced details such as pricing, specifications, or purchase options.  
2. Implement a registration prompt when a guest user attempts to access product details, offering the option to register or log in before proceeding.  
3. Restrict product detail access to registered users only, but provide a summary preview or limited information in the search results for all users.

Original_FR: A user can then select the product and view the details.
Conflicting_FR: Product detail viewing shall be available to standard users for a single 30-second preview per day; after the preview expires, standard users are blocked for the remainder of the day.
Resolution:
1. Clarify that Requirement A applies to all users but is subject to the access restrictions defined in Requirement B, ensuring standard users can only view details within the 30-second daily preview window.  
2. Introduce a tiered access system where "select and view details" is unrestricted for premium users, while standard users are governed by the preview rule in Requirement B.  
3. Modify Requirement A to explicitly state that product detail viewing is conditional, linking it to the preview mechanism in Requirement B to avoid ambiguity.

Original_FR: A user can then select the product and view the details.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall automatically display product details without requiring explicit user selection.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a user preference setting that allows the user to choose between automatic display or manual selection for viewing product details.
2. Implement a hybrid approach where product details are automatically displayed for first-time or highlighted items, but require explicit selection for other products.
3. Reframe the requirements by making Requirement A apply to a detailed view page, while Requirement B applies to a quick preview feature on the main listing.

Original_FR: A user may reorder routes or remove flight plans assigned to a UAV.
Conflicting_FR: Only flight controllers shall have permission to reorder or remove UAV flight plans.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the term "user" in Requirement A to explicitly exclude flight controllers, specifying that it refers to non-controller operators or a different user role, thereby aligning with Requirement B's permission restriction.

2. Introduce a role-based access control system where "user" is defined as a broader category, and then assign the permission to reorder or remove flight plans exclusively to the "flight controller" role, ensuring both requirements are satisfied through clear role distinctions.

3. Revise Requirement A to state that a user may *request* reordering or removal of flight plans, but actual execution requires approval or action by a flight controller, maintaining user interaction while upholding the permission rule in Requirement B.

Original_FR: A user must be logged on to be able to view or edit any medical information.
Conflicting_FR: Users shall be able to view general medical summaries without logging in.
Resolution:
1. Redefine "general medical summaries" as non-sensitive, publicly accessible information that is explicitly excluded from the scope of Requirement A, thereby allowing anonymous access to that specific category only.

2. Implement a tiered authentication system where a minimal "guest" login (e.g., using a temporary or low-privilege account) is required to view summaries, satisfying Requirement A's intent of traceability while meeting Requirement B's ease of access.

3. Split the functionality: allow users to view anonymized, aggregate medical summaries without login, but require full authentication to view or edit any personal or identifiable medical information.

Original_FR: Accepted input for price and destination search DESC: Integers should be accepted as input when a user searches by price or destination. If the system receives an invalid input the user should be informed and prompted to insert an accepted input.
Conflicting_FR: If the price and destination search is initiated, the THEMAS system shall accept only alphanumeric strings as input and shall reject all numerical entries, returning an error code to the calling service.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the input type by specifying separate fields: allow numeric input for price (Requirement A) and alphanumeric for destination (Requirement B), with clear validation and error messages for each field.  
2. Introduce a unified input format, such as alphanumeric strings that can include numbers for price (e.g., "100USD" or "Paris123"), and update both requirements to accept this hybrid format with consistent validation rules.  
3. Define context-based acceptance: if the search is triggered via a user interface, follow Requirement A (integers allowed); if via an API/service call, follow Requirement B (alphanumeric only, with error codes).

Original_FR: Accepted input for price and destination search: Integers should be accepted.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall accept both integers and floating-point numbers for price and destination searches.
Resolution:
1. Clarify and merge the requirements to state that the system shall accept both integers and floating-point numbers for price and destination searches, as Requirement B already encompasses Requirement A.
2. Update Requirement A to explicitly include floating-point numbers, ensuring consistency with Requirement B and eliminating redundancy.
3. If there is a specific need to treat integers and floating-point numbers differently, specify distinct validation or processing rules for each data type in a unified requirement.

Original_FR: Add a new dish — Given the administrator is logged in, When the administrator creates a new dish, Then the new dish should be added.
Conflicting_FR: Only the restaurant owner shall be allowed to add a new dish, not the administrator.
Resolution:
1. Clarify roles by redefining "administrator" in Requirement A to specifically mean "restaurant owner" or a designated owner-level role, ensuring both requirements align on who is authorized to add dishes.  
2. Introduce role-based permissions where the system distinguishes between a general administrator and a restaurant owner, allowing only the owner to add dishes while administrators handle other tasks.  
3. Revise Requirement A to include a precondition that the user must be verified as the restaurant owner, explicitly stating: "Given the restaurant owner is logged in..." to eliminate ambiguity.

Original_FR: Add a new restaurant owner — Given the administrator is logged in...
Conflicting_FR: Only the system super-administrator shall be allowed to create new restaurant owners.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the term "administrator" in Requirement A to explicitly mean "system super-administrator," ensuring both requirements align on the same role and authority level.
2. Introduce a hierarchical role system where a general administrator can request to add a restaurant owner, but final approval must be granted by the system super-administrator.
3. Revise Requirement A to specify that only the system super-administrator can perform the action, removing any ambiguity and directly harmonizing it with Requirement B.

Original_FR: Add a new restaurant owner Given the administrator is logged in When the administrator creates a new restaurant owner Then the new restaurant owner should be added
Conflicting_FR: Creation of new restaurant owners shall be allowed for SUPER_ADMIN only during a single 60-second annual provisioning window; outside that 60-second window no creations are permitted.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to include a precondition that the administrator must have the SUPER_ADMIN role and that the action must occur within the annual 60-second provisioning window, ensuring both functional and security constraints are satisfied.  
2. Update Requirement B to allow creation of new restaurant owners by any logged-in administrator, but restrict the timing to the 60-second window only, removing the role restriction if role-based access is less critical than the time constraint.  
3. Introduce a two-step verification process where any administrator can initiate the creation, but final approval or execution requires SUPER_ADMIN authorization and must occur within the designated annual window, combining both requirements.

Original_FR: Add a new restaurant type — Given the administrator is logged in...
Conflicting_FR: Restaurant owners shall be allowed to define new restaurant types independently.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a hierarchical permission system where administrators can create global restaurant types, while restaurant owners can define custom types for their own establishments only, with a clear naming convention to avoid conflicts.

2. Implement a proposal and approval workflow where restaurant owners can suggest new restaurant types, which are then reviewed and officially added by administrators, ensuring consistency while granting autonomy.

3. Define distinct scopes: Requirement A applies to system-wide, standardized restaurant categories, while Requirement B allows owners to create tags or subcategories specific to their restaurant's internal use, not visible in public filters.

Original_FR: Add restaurant information — Given the administrator is logged in...
Conflicting_FR: Only the restaurant owner shall be permitted to add or modify restaurant information.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the role definitions by specifying that "administrator" in Requirement A refers exclusively to a designated "restaurant owner" administrator, thereby aligning both requirements to restrict actions to restaurant owners only.
2. Introduce a tiered access system where general administrators can view restaurant information but only restaurant owners have the authority to add or modify it, explicitly updating Requirement A to reflect this limitation.
3. Revise Requirement A to include a conditional clause, such as "Given the administrator is logged in and is the verified owner of the restaurant," to directly incorporate the ownership restriction from Requirement B.

Original_FR: Adding information with mandatory and optional fields — Given the restaurant owner has filled in the mandatory fields...
Conflicting_FR: The system shall reject submissions containing optional fields until verified by an administrator.
Resolution:
1. Allow optional fields to be submitted but flag them for administrator verification before the information is published or used in downstream processes.  
2. Split the submission process into two steps: mandatory fields are submitted first, and optional fields can be added later after administrator approval of the initial submission.  
3. Implement role-based permissions so that trusted users (e.g., verified restaurant owners) can submit optional fields without verification, while new or unverified users require administrative review.

Original_FR: Adding information with mandatory fields — Given the restaurant owner has filled in the mandatory fields...
Conflicting_FR: Only administrators shall be permitted to add new restaurant information.
Resolution:
1. Clarify roles by redefining "restaurant owner" in Requirement A as a type of administrator with specific privileges, ensuring only authorized users can add information.  
2. Modify Requirement A to state that after an administrator adds the restaurant, the owner can only edit or complete mandatory fields, not create new entries.  
3. Introduce a two-step process: administrators create the restaurant entry, then the designated owner is granted access to fill in mandatory details.

Original_FR: Adding information with mandatory fields Given the restaurant owner has filled in the mandatory fields of the form When the restaurant owner submits the form Then the information about the restaurant should be added
Conflicting_FR: New restaurant submissions shall be auto-approved if the regional manager does not review them within 2 seconds of submission; no human approval is then required.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a conditional workflow where the auto-approval timer starts only after the mandatory fields are validated and the form is successfully submitted, ensuring Requirement A is met before Requirement B's timer is triggered.  
2. Modify Requirement B to apply only after a brief system verification of mandatory fields, adding a step that confirms the submission is complete and valid before the 2-second auto-approval window begins.  
3. Clarify that Requirement A's "added" means the data is stored in a pending state, and Requirement B's auto-approval then finalizes the submission if no manager review occurs within 2 seconds, separating data entry from approval.

Original_FR: after every user request, new maps are needed to be loaded.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall cache maps and reuse them instead of loading new ones after every request.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a conditional caching strategy where maps are loaded only if not already cached, and reused from cache when available, satisfying both requirements by loading "new maps" only when necessary.  
2. Define a time-based or event-driven cache refresh policy, such that maps are reloaded after a specific period or upon user request for updates, balancing fresh data with performance.  
3. Clarify the term "new maps" in Requirement A to specify whether it refers to newly requested maps or freshly loaded data, aligning it with Requirement B’s caching intent.

Original_FR: After installing the Application from iTunes or the App store the user will be asked to login.
Conflicting_FR: Application launch without login shall be allowed only once per device ever; all subsequent launches shall require full authentication.
Resolution:
1. Clarify that Requirement A's login prompt occurs only after the first launch, aligning with Requirement B's allowance of one launch without authentication per device.
2. Modify Requirement A to specify that the login prompt appears on the second launch onward, ensuring it does not conflict with the single allowed launch without login.
3. Introduce a grace period or tutorial mode during the first launch that defers the login requirement until after initial setup, satisfying both requirements.

Original_FR: After installing the Application from iTunes or the App Store, the user will be asked to login.
Conflicting_FR: The user shall be automatically logged in after installation without a manual login step.
Resolution:
1. Implement a "first-time user" detection that automatically logs in after installation using stored device credentials or a one-time token, while requiring manual login only for subsequent app launches or reinstallations.

2. Introduce a user preference setting during the installation process, allowing the user to choose between automatic login (e.g., via biometrics or saved credentials) or a manual login for enhanced security.

3. Use a hybrid approach where the app automatically logs the user in after installation if they are already authenticated on the device (e.g., via Apple ID or Google account), otherwise prompt for manual login.

Original_FR: After selecting one of the months, the system shall generate the ASCII report file.
Conflicting_FR: The system shall generate a PDF report instead of an ASCII report file.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the report format requirement by specifying that the system shall generate a PDF report by default, but allow an optional ASCII report export upon user selection.  
2. Modify Requirement A to state that the system shall generate a report file, with the format (PDF or ASCII) determined by a user-configurable setting or system preference.  
3. Combine both requirements by having the system generate both a PDF report for viewing and an ASCII report file for data export, with the month selection triggering both outputs.

Original_FR: After selecting one of the months, the system shall generate the ASCII report file.
Conflicting_FR: After selecting one of the months, the system shall generate a PDF report file.
Resolution:
1. Combine both outputs by having the system generate a single report that can be exported in both ASCII and PDF formats, allowing the user to choose the format after selection.
2. Prioritize one format as the default (e.g., PDF) and offer ASCII as an optional export, updating Requirement A to reflect it as an alternative rather than the primary output.
3. Clarify the stakeholder needs to determine if both reports are necessary for different purposes; if so, modify the requirements to specify that both files are generated simultaneously upon month selection.

Original_FR: Agenda is restricted to what is available in the feeds on the list of agenda RSS-feeds.
Conflicting_FR: Agenda items may include externally sourced content beyond the RSS feeds.
Resolution:
1. Define a primary and secondary content hierarchy where the agenda is primarily populated from the RSS feeds, but allows for manual addition or linking of external content as supplemental items, clearly marked as such.  
2. Modify Requirement A to specify a core agenda sourced from RSS feeds, while Requirement B is rephrased to allow optional external content only when approved or tagged separately, ensuring both can coexist without overlap.  
3. Introduce a configuration setting or user role permission that toggles between a strict RSS-only agenda mode and a flexible mode that permits external content, depending on user needs or context.

Original_FR: All information must be updatable on the website.
Conflicting_FR: Only administrators shall be able to update website information; general users shall not.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the scope of "All information" in Requirement A by specifying that it refers to the capability of the system to allow updates, while restricting actual update permissions to administrators as per Requirement B.
2. Introduce role-based access control, where administrators have full update permissions, and general users can submit update requests that require administrative approval before being applied.
3. Revise Requirement A to state that "All information must be updatable by authorized personnel on the website," aligning it with Requirement B's permission constraints.

Original_FR: All information must be viewable on the website.
Conflicting_FR: Certain sensitive information shall not be viewable on the website.
Resolution:
1. Define and categorize "sensitive information" explicitly, then modify Requirement A to state that all *non-sensitive* information must be viewable on the website.
2. Implement role-based access controls, allowing Requirement A to apply to general users while Requirement B restricts sensitive data to authorized users only.
3. Replace "viewable" with more precise terms, such as "accessible" or "displayed," and specify that sensitive information may be listed on the site but its detailed content requires a separate secure authentication step.

Original_FR: All letter requests must be formatted according to guidelines specified by the Print Letter Utility system.
Conflicting_FR: Letter requests may follow either the system format or user-defined templates.
Resolution:
1. Clarify that the system format is the default and mandatory baseline, while user-defined templates must be validated against the Print Letter Utility guidelines before approval.  
2. Introduce a tiered system where standard letters follow the system format, while custom letters can use user-defined templates if they meet predefined compliance criteria.  
3. Update Requirement B to specify that user-defined templates are only permitted for non-standard letters and must still adhere to core formatting rules from the Print Letter Utility guidelines.

Original_FR: All on-line messages must have the ability to include card issuer discretionary data from the CEP card.
Conflicting_FR: Online messages shall exclude issuer discretionary data for security reasons.
Resolution:
1. Clarify the term "security reasons" in Requirement B to determine if it applies to all data or only specific sensitive fields, then adjust Requirement A to exclude only those flagged items.
2. Introduce a conditional rule where issuer discretionary data is included in online messages only when encrypted or tokenized, satisfying both the functional need and security concerns.
3. Define distinct message types or contexts—such as "administrative messages" that include the data versus "transactional messages" that exclude it—and specify which requirement applies to each.

Original_FR: All Pilot communication with the UAV shall require secure connections.
Conflicting_FR: Pilots may use unsecured connections for training flights.
Resolution:
1. Revise Requirement B to specify that unsecured connections are only permitted in a controlled, isolated training environment that is physically and logically separated from operational networks.
2. Add a new requirement that defines "training flights" as non-operational exercises conducted within a secure, simulated environment where no real mission data is transmitted.
3. Introduce a conditional clause to Requirement A, such as "except during authorized training exercises conducted under predefined safety and security protocols," and document those protocols in a separate security policy.

Original_FR: All registered customer information shall be stored and maintained in the database even after account cancellation.
Conflicting_FR: Customer data shall be permanently deleted once the account is canceled.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a data retention policy that anonymizes or archives customer information after cancellation, keeping it for legal or analytical purposes without storing personally identifiable data permanently.  
2. Implement a tiered deletion process where certain non-sensitive data is retained for a set period (e.g., 30 days) before permanent deletion, while sensitive data is removed immediately upon cancellation.  
3. Clarify the scope by separating "customer information" into transactional/legal records (kept) and personal identifiers (deleted), ensuring Requirement A applies to anonymized logs and Requirement B to private user data.

Original_FR: All searchable product-specific metadata in MODIS standard products shall be formatted as specified in DID 311.
Conflicting_FR: MODIS metadata may follow updated format guidelines replacing DID 311.
Resolution:
1. Revise Requirement A to specify that product metadata shall follow the current format guidelines, which are initially defined by DID 311 but may be updated, and maintain a controlled reference document for the active standard.
2. Clarify Requirement B by adding a condition that any updated format guidelines must be formally approved and documented before replacing DID 311, ensuring backward compatibility or a defined transition period.
3. Merge both requirements into a single statement: "MODIS product metadata shall adhere to the officially approved format standard, which is currently DID 311, with provisions for adopting future updates through a controlled change process."

Original_FR: All streaming movie sales will be logged in the database and accessible by the administrator.
Conflicting_FR: Sales logs shall be accessible to both administrators and authorized auditors.
Resolution:
1. Clarify access levels by specifying that administrators have full access (including logging and viewing), while authorized auditors have read-only access to sales logs.
2. Define "authorized auditors" explicitly in the requirements to ensure they are distinct from administrators and have access only to logs, not to logging functions.
3. Separate the logging and access requirements: keep Requirement A for logging and administrative access, and modify Requirement B to state that sales logs (once created) are accessible to both administrators and authorized auditors.

Original_FR: All streaming movie sales will be logged in the database and accessible by the administrator.
Conflicting_FR: Streaming movie sales logs shall be viewable by admins for a single continuous 10-second interval each month; for all other times only internal audit may view them.
Resolution:
1. Modify Requirement A to specify that administrator access to streaming movie sales logs is limited to a designated 10-second window each month, aligning with Requirement B’s access restriction.  
2. Introduce a role-based access control system where “administrator” is subdivided into roles: one with continuous audit access and another with restricted monthly access, clearly assigning permissions per Requirement B.  
3. Clarify that “accessible by the administrator” in Requirement A refers to metadata or summary reports only, while detailed logs are restricted as per Requirement B, ensuring both requirements are satisfied without overlap.

Original_FR: All UAV data transmission shall be secure against unauthorized access.
Conflicting_FR: UAV data may be transmitted in plaintext during test operations.
Resolution:
1. Introduce a conditional clause in Requirement A to explicitly exclude test operations, such as: "All UAV data transmission shall be secure against unauthorized access, except during authorized test operations where plaintext transmission is permitted."
2. Define a separate, secure test environment or isolated network for test operations in Requirement B, ensuring that plaintext transmission is only allowed within that controlled environment and does not compromise overall system security.
3. Establish a procedural safeguard requiring explicit authorization and logging for any plaintext transmission during tests, with a clear definition of what constitutes a "test operation" to prevent misuse.

Original_FR: All UAV data transmission shall be secure from unauthorized access.
Conflicting_FR: UAV data may be broadcast without encryption for telemetry testing.
Resolution:
1. Define specific operational modes or contexts where each requirement applies, such as designating "telemetry testing" as a controlled, isolated environment where unencrypted broadcast is permitted, while mandating encryption for all other operational scenarios.
2. Introduce conditional encryption by requiring that any broadcast data for testing purposes be anonymized or stripped of sensitive information, ensuring that even unencrypted transmissions do not expose critical data to unauthorized access.
3. Implement a procedural safeguard requiring explicit authorization and logging for any unencrypted broadcast, coupled with real-time monitoring to detect and prevent unauthorized access during testing phases.

Original_FR: All users shall be able to zoom into images using standard gestures.
Conflicting_FR: Only premium users shall be able to zoom into images.
Resolution:
1. Introduce tiered functionality: Allow all users to zoom into images using standard gestures, but restrict premium users to advanced zoom features (e.g., higher resolution, pinch-to-zoom with enhanced detail, or zoom beyond a certain level).

2. Implement a freemium model: Grant all users basic zoom capabilities (e.g., up to 2x zoom) for free, while reserving unlimited or high-quality zoom exclusively for premium users.

3. Redefine user roles: Adjust Requirement A to specify that "all registered users" can zoom, then create a separate category (e.g., guest or trial users) with limited or no zoom access, aligning premium status with full zoom privileges.

Original_FR: All users should be able to go back to the product category page via the back button.
Conflicting_FR: Only registered users shall have access to the back navigation feature.
Resolution:
1. Redefine the back button's behavior so that it is universally available but leads to different destinations: for registered users, it navigates to the product category page; for unregistered users, it redirects to a login or informational page with an option to register.

2. Implement a tiered navigation system where all users see a basic back button (e.g., browser back or a simple "previous page" link), while registered users have access to an enhanced back navigation feature that includes shortcuts or history tracking.

3. Clarify the requirements by specifying that "access to the back navigation feature" in Requirement B refers to an advanced, persistent navigation panel, while Requirement A's back button is a standard, one-step browser-style control available to everyone.

Original_FR: All users should be able to navigate using the sub-navigation icons to access PDFs, videos, and contacts.
Conflicting_FR: Only authenticated users shall access the catalogue PDF and 360 videos.
Resolution:
1. Implement role-based access control where sub-navigation icons are visible to all users, but PDFs and 360 videos are only accessible after authentication, with clear indicators for restricted content.
